title: 'Pulse completely dropped': What we know about Prateek Yadav’s death so far
description: Prateek Yadav, step-brother of Akhilesh Yadav and husband of BJP leader Aparna Yadav, died at 38 in Lucknow under mysterious circumstances. Brought to Civil Hospital by Aparna's brother, doctors declared him dead on arrival. The exact cause of death is under investigation pending a postmortem examination.

Gill posted “Dil abhi bhi sache, aur ghar bhi kache” on Instagram, referencing an old joke about SRH’s poor record against GT. On the field, half-centuries from Sai Sudharsan and Washington Sundar helped Gujarat dominate Hyderabad in Ahmedabad.

Akhilesh Yadav's stepbrother, husband of BJP leader Aparna Yadav, Prateek dies at 38
description: Prateek Yadav, 38, stepbrother of Akhilesh Yadav and husband of BJP leader Aparna Yadav, was found dead at Lucknow's Civil Hospital early Wednesday. Doctors declared him brought dead. The younger son of the late Mulayam Singh Yadav, Prateek was not involved in politics. A post-mortem is underway to determine the cause of his demise.

description: A 33-year-old Public Works Department engineer ended his life by jumping from Devarayanadurga hilltop in Tumakuru district. The engineer, Manju Prasad, was reportedly upset after his mother's passing last year. He left home on Monday, telling his wife he was going to his office. He visited a relative before proceeding to the hilltop.

description: Another woman has died from post-cesarean section complications at Kota's New Medical College Hospital, marking the fourth fatality in a week. Pinki Mahawar, 30, succumbed after being transferred from JK Lon Hospital, with her husband alleging a delayed referral. The incident has led to protests and calls for accountability regarding safety protocols in government hospitals.

title: A US family sold their company for $1.7 bn and gave 540 workers $240 mn of it
description: A US-based businessman, Graham Walker, gifted his 540 employees a staggering $240 million from the $1.7 billion sale of his family's company, Fibrebond Corp. This generous act, where workers received an average of $443,000, stemmed from a condition Walker imposed before the sale to power-management giant Eaton.
